codeReview.summary: The backend portion of the tool call routing was implemented successfully (backend -> WebSocket), but the frontend portion (WebSocket -> React Renderer -> IPC -> Electron Main) was completely omitted. The worker did not fulfill the entire feature description and did not perform the end-to-end verification step.

issues.description: Missing implementation. The feature requires routing tool calls from the WebSocket to the React Renderer and then via IPC to Electron Main. No frontend code was modified to handle incoming `tool_call` WebSocket messages or pass them to Electron Main.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `midscene-tool-call` feature spanned both backend and frontend domains (routing from backend through React to Electron Main), but was assigned to the `backend-worker`. The worker strictly followed its skill bounds (modifying only Python backend files) and therefore left the feature incomplete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows the worker only edited `src/app/api/v1/endpoints/live.py` and `tests/test_midscene_tool.py`. They attempted to build the frontend at the end but made no code changes to route the tool call in React or Electron Main.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker failed to perform the requested verification step ('Trigger tool call and verify it reaches Electron Main'). They attempted to run the backend and frontend manually using commands like `$env:PORT=8000; uv run uvicorn ...` and `cd frontend && npm install`, but failed to retrieve background logs or verify the app running, relying only on a mocked unit test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows failed commands (`Get-Process -Id 71244` and `Error reading file: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access 'C:\\Users\\tazzo\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\droid-bg-1772798147719.out'`). The worker eventually abandoned end-to-end verification.

summary: Review failed: The implementation successfully exposes the `execute_midscene_action` tool in the Python backend and routes it over the WebSocket, but completely omits the required React Renderer and Electron IPC routing. The worker did not verify that the tool call reaches Electron Main.
